phase_statuses.title: Backend WS04 Ledger and Workflow API
phase_statuses.objective: Deliver WS04 backend data model and workflow transitions including change requests, plan items, reminders, cross-department confirmation, and closure writeback fields.
phase_statuses.evidence_expectation: API unit tests cover happy-path and guard-path transitions, with execution log entries listing commands and changed paths.

acceptance_criteria.text: Backend exposes `change_control` APIs for create/list/get workflow requests with strict required-field validation.
Backend supports plan-item create/update/list and enforces parent request state rules.
Backend supports due reminder dispatch using existing inbox notification structure (no custom notification schema).
Backend supports cross-department confirmation and transition to closable state.
Backend close operation records ledger writeback fields, controlled revision references, and closure metadata.

phase_statuses.title: Frontend WS04 Page and API Client
phase_statuses.objective: Expose WS04 workflow from `/quality-system/change-control` without changing route registry wiring.
phase_statuses.evidence_expectation: frontend unit tests validate rendering and API interaction behavior.

acceptance_criteria.text: `/quality-system/change-control` renders WS04 feature page instead of placeholder shell content.
Frontend change-control API client maps backend response contracts and propagates backend errors.
WS04 page supports at least create request, add plan item, dispatch reminders, confirm, and close actions.

phase_statuses.title: Verification and Task Artifacts Completion
phase_statuses.objective: Validate WS04 implementation with unit tests and update task evidence artifacts.
phase_statuses.evidence_expectation: concrete command outputs summarized in test report and referenced from state evidence entries.

acceptance_criteria.text: Backend WS04 tests pass in local repo test run.
Frontend WS04 tests pass in local repo test run.
`execution-log.md` and `test-report.md` contain acceptance-id traceable evidence for P1/P2/P3 criteria.
